classDescription: A primal front-line warrior who channels Rage into durability, pressure, and battlefield momentum.
roleIdentity: Front-line bruiser: enter Rage early, trade safely with resistance, and control melee tempo through relentless pressure.

barbarian-rage.name: Rage
barbarian-rage.summary: Enter a rage to add bonus melee damage, gain physical resistance, and unlock Barbarian pressure turns.
barbarian-rage.description:
As a bonus action, you enter a Rage that turns you into a durable melee threat for up to 10 minutes.

While raging, your Strength-based melee weapon attacks gain a Rage Damage bonus: +2 at levels 1-8, +3 at levels 9-15, and +4 at levels 16-20. Rage damage applies on each qualifying hit.

Rage also grants resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage, and it fuels many Barbarian and subclass features.

Extra Attack is a separate class feature: at Barbarian level 5, your Attack action makes 2 attacks instead of 1. Rage does not grant Extra Attack by itself.

Regain expended Rage uses on a Long Rest.
barbarian-rage.duration: Up to 10 minutes; ends early if you are incapacitated or if your turn passes without attacking or forcing a save.
barbarian-rage.recovery: Regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
barbarian-rage.effect: Strength-based melee weapon attacks gain Rage damage (+2 at levels 1-8, +3 at 9-15, +4 at 16-20) and you gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage while raging.

barbarian-unarmored-defense.name: Unarmored Defense
barbarian-unarmored-defense.summary: Your Armor Class comes from your body and stats instead of needing armor.
barbarian-unarmored-defense.description:
While you are not wearing any armor, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.

This replaces the standard AC calculation — it does not stack with other Unarmored Defense features or mage armor.
barbarian-unarmored-defense.effect: While not wearing armor, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can still use a shield.

barbarian-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.name: Weapon Mastery (2 weapons)
barbarian-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.summary: Use the Mastery property of 2 proficient weapons, applying special effects like Push, Topple, Cleave, or Vex on hits.
barbarian-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.description:
You can use the Mastery property of 2 weapon types you are proficient with. You can change your chosen weapons when you finish a Long Rest.

Weapon Mastery properties add tactical effects to hits: Cleave hits an additional adjacent creature, Push shoves the target, Topple can knock a creature Prone, Vex grants Advantage on your next attack against the same target, Slow reduces the target's speed, Graze deals damage even on a miss, Nick allows an extra attack with the light property, and Sap gives Disadvantage on the target's next attack.

barbarian-reckless-attack.name: Reckless Attack
barbarian-reckless-attack.summary: Trade defense for accuracy by going all-in on your offense.
barbarian-reckless-attack.description:
When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you Advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn. During the same period, attack rolls against you have Advantage.

You do not need to decide before you roll — you can choose after seeing the result of the roll only if you haven't yet decided to attack normally.
barbarian-reckless-attack.trigger: When you make your first attack on your turn
barbarian-reckless-attack.effect: You gain Advantage on Strength-based melee attack rolls this turn, and attacks against you have Advantage until your next turn.

barbarian-danger-sense.name: Danger Sense
barbarian-danger-sense.summary: You react quickly to visible threats and effects that can be dodged.
barbarian-danger-sense.description:
You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects that you can see, such as traps and spells.

To gain this benefit you must not be Blinded, Deafened, or Incapacitated.
barbarian-danger-sense.effect: You have Advantage on Dexterity saves against visible effects, such as traps and spells.

barbarian-primal-path-subclass.name: Primal Path (Subclass)
barbarian-primal-path-subclass.summary: Choose your path and unlock a stronger identity for the rest of the build.
barbarian-primal-path-subclass.description:
At 3rd level you choose a Primal Path that shapes the rest of your Barbarian build and progression. Each path grants unique features at levels 3, 6, 10, and 14, defining your combat style and thematic identity.

Options include the Path of the Berserker (relentless melee aggression), Path of the Wild Heart (primal beast affinity), Path of the World Tree (cosmic Yggdrasil magic), and Path of the Zealot (divine fury that defies death), among others.

barbarian-primal-knowledge.name: Primal Knowledge
barbarian-primal-knowledge.summary: Your barbarian instincts now support exploration and survival as well as combat.
barbarian-primal-knowledge.description:
You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice from the Barbarian skill list: Animal Handling, Athletics, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, or Survival.

At 7th level, when you enter your Rage, you can channel it into your body and mind. Until your Rage ends, you can add your Rage Damage bonus to Strength ability checks and Constitution ability checks.

barbarian-ability-score-improvement.name: Ability Score Improvement
barbarian-ability-score-improvement.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
barbarian-ability-score-improvement.description:
You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. No score can exceed 20 using this feature.

Alternatively, you can forgo the ability score increases to gain a feat of your choice for which you qualify.

barbarian-extra-attack.name: Extra Attack
barbarian-extra-attack.summary: When you take the Attack action, you can now make up to 2 attacks within that action.
barbarian-extra-attack.description:
Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

This does not apply to Bonus Action attacks or opportunity attacks — only to the Attack action itself.

barbarian-fast-movement.name: Fast Movement
barbarian-fast-movement.summary: You move faster than most front-line warriors, helping you stick to priority targets.
barbarian-fast-movement.description:
Your speed increases by 10 feet while you are not wearing Heavy Armor.

This bonus applies to your base walking speed and stacks with other speed increases.

barbarian-subclass-feature.name: Subclass Feature
barbarian-subclass-feature.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
barbarian-subclass-feature.description: Your chosen Primal Path grants you an additional feature at this level. The exact ability depends on your path — check your subclass for the full details of this unlock.

barbarian-feral-instinct.name: Feral Instinct
barbarian-feral-instinct.summary: Your battlefield instincts make it harder to catch you slow or unready.
barbarian-feral-instinct.description:
Your instincts are so honed that you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

Additionally, if you are Surprised at the start of combat and are not Incapacitated, you can act normally on your first turn as long as you enter your Rage before doing anything else on that turn.

barbarian-instinctive-pounce.name: Instinctive Pounce
barbarian-instinctive-pounce.summary: When you ignite your combat state, you also gain immediate pressure and repositioning power.
barbarian-instinctive-pounce.description:
As part of the Bonus Action you take to enter your Rage, you can move up to half your Speed.

This movement does not provoke Opportunity Attacks and lets you close distance or reposition before your first attack of the encounter.

barbarian-ability-score-improvement-2.name: Ability Score Improvement
barbarian-ability-score-improvement-2.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
barbarian-ability-score-improvement-2.description:
You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. No score can exceed 20 using this feature.

Alternatively, you can forgo the ability score increases to gain a feat of your choice for which you qualify.

barbarian-brutal-strike-1d10-effect.name: Brutal Strike (1d10 + effect)
barbarian-brutal-strike-1d10-effect.summary: When you attack recklessly, you can forgo Advantage on one Strength attack to add 1d10 damage and apply a rider effect.
barbarian-brutal-strike-1d10-effect.description:
When you use Reckless Attack, you can forgo Advantage on one of the attack rolls to make it a Brutal Strike. If that attack hits, it deals an extra 1d10 damage.

You also apply one of the following Brutal Strike effects to the hit:

**Forceful Blow.** The target is pushed up to 15 feet straight away from you. You can then move up to 15 feet straight toward the target without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

**Hamstring Blow.** The target's Speed is reduced by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.
barbarian-brutal-strike-1d10-effect.trigger: When you use Reckless Attack and hit with a Strength-based attack
barbarian-brutal-strike-1d10-effect.effect: Adds extra brutality to a successful hit, with stronger payoff as the barbarian levels.

barbarian-subclass-feature-2.name: Subclass Feature
barbarian-subclass-feature-2.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
barbarian-subclass-feature-2.description: Your chosen Primal Path grants you an additional feature at this level. The exact ability depends on your path — check your subclass for the full details of this unlock.

barbarian-relentless-rage.name: Relentless Rage
barbarian-relentless-rage.summary: Dropping you is much harder once your fury is fully awake.
barbarian-relentless-rage.description:
If you drop to 0 Hit Points while you're raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, you drop to 1 Hit Point instead.

Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. The DC resets back to 10 when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

barbarian-ability-score-improvement-3.name: Ability Score Improvement
barbarian-ability-score-improvement-3.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
barbarian-ability-score-improvement-3.description:
You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. No score can exceed 20 using this feature.

Alternatively, you can forgo the ability score increases to gain a feat of your choice for which you qualify.

barbarian-brutal-strike-2-effects.name: Brutal Strike (2 effects)
barbarian-brutal-strike-2-effects.summary: Your Brutal Strike package improves with stronger riders and better turn impact.
barbarian-brutal-strike-2-effects.description:
Your Brutal Strike improves. When you use it, you can now apply two different Brutal Strike effects (instead of one) to the hit. You still deal only the extra 1d10 damage once, but both effects apply.

The available effects remain Forceful Blow (push 15 ft + move 15 ft toward target) and Hamstring Blow (target's speed reduced by 15 ft until your next turn).
barbarian-brutal-strike-2-effects.effect: Adds extra brutality to a successful hit, with stronger payoff as the barbarian levels.

barbarian-subclass-feature-3.name: Subclass Feature
barbarian-subclass-feature-3.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
barbarian-subclass-feature-3.description: Your chosen Primal Path grants you an additional feature at this level. The exact ability depends on your path — check your subclass for the full details of this unlock.

barbarian-persistent-rage.name: Persistent Rage
barbarian-persistent-rage.summary: Your rage is easier to maintain, making the combat state more reliable over long exchanges.
barbarian-persistent-rage.description:
Your Rage is so fierce that it ends early only if you fall Unconscious or choose to end it.

You no longer need to attack or deal damage each round to sustain your Rage — it persists automatically regardless of what you do on your turn.

barbarian-ability-score-improvement-4.name: Ability Score Improvement
barbarian-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
barbarian-ability-score-improvement-4.description:
You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. No score can exceed 20 using this feature.

Alternatively, you can forgo the ability score increases to gain a feat of your choice for which you qualify.

barbarian-brutal-strike-3d10.name: Brutal Strike (3d10)
barbarian-brutal-strike-3d10.summary: Your Brutal Strike extra damage increases to 3d10 when you land the strike.
barbarian-brutal-strike-3d10.description:
The extra damage of your Brutal Strike increases to 3d10. You still apply two effects when using Brutal Strike, and the Forceful Blow and Hamstring Blow effects remain unchanged.

This is also the level where your Rage Damage bonus increases to +4 (from +3).
barbarian-brutal-strike-3d10.effect: Adds extra brutality to a successful hit, with stronger payoff as the barbarian levels.

barbarian-indomitable-might.name: Indomitable Might
barbarian-indomitable-might.summary: Your physical strength becomes so reliable that brute-force checks stop feeling swingy.
barbarian-indomitable-might.description:
If the total for a Strength check is less than your Strength score, you can use your Strength score in place of the total.

This means your Strength checks can never result in a number lower than your Strength score itself — a floor, not a bonus.

barbarian-epic-boon.name: Epic Boon
barbarian-epic-boon.summary: Gain an endgame reward that pushes the build into truly legendary territory.
barbarian-epic-boon.description:
At 19th level you gain an Epic Boon feat of your choice, or another feat you qualify for. Epic Boon feats are high-power rewards available only at this tier.

Examples include Epic Boon of Combat Prowess (reroll a missed melee attack once per Short Rest), Epic Boon of Dimensional Travel (Misty Step as a Bonus Action once per Short Rest), and Epic Boon of the Night Spirit (gain Darkvision 60 ft and advantage on Stealth checks in dim light or darkness).

barbarian-primal-champion.name: Primal Champion
barbarian-primal-champion.summary: Your body reaches a legendary peak of raw physical dominance.
barbarian-primal-champion.description:
You embody the power of the wilds. Your Strength and Constitution scores each increase by 4, and their maximums each increase by 4 (to a maximum of 24).

This applies immediately and affects all derived statistics — attack rolls, damage, Constitution saves, hit points gained from future levels, and your AC if using Unarmored Defense.

progressionSummary.summary: Rage, Unarmored Defense, Weapon Mastery (2 weapons).

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your melee attacks and grappling
buildTips.secondaryReason: boosts your HP and keeps you standing while raging
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: you still need decent initiative and armor class even as a bruiser
buildTips.coreLoop: Open hard fights with Rage, then keep attacking every round to maintain pressure and value from Rage-linked features.
buildTips.primaryStats: Strength powers attack pressure; Constitution powers durability and Unarmored Defense.
buildTips.gear: Use martial melee weapons, carry javelins/handaxes for reach turns, and consider shield vs two-handed based on role.
